text: Continuous Integration is a software development practice where team members frequently merge their code changes into a shared repository. Each integration is verified by an automated build and running tests, which helps catch issues early. This process is managed through CI pipelines, which are automated sequences that build the code, run tests, and report results. By detecting problems quickly, developers can fix them before they become larger issues. This approach improves team collaboration and code quality, as changes are tested and validated continuously rather than waiting for a final release.
